page: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highmem-96-metal is a bare-metal server instance belonging to the c4a server family. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 CPU architecture. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. This configuration does not include local NVMe SSD storage or integrated graphics processing units. Additionally, it does not bundle any complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

With its high memory density of 8.0 GB per core, this bare-metal instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads that require dedicated physical resources without hypervisor overhead. The absence of local storage means all storage operations rely on network-attached block storage, which is a key architectural tradeoff to consider for I/O-heavy applications. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal delivery model provides direct hardware access, maximizing resource utilization for large-scale enterprise applications. This server is suited for large databases, in-memory analytics, enterprise resource planning systems, and memory-heavy containerized deployments.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highmem-96-metal is an x86_64 bare-metal server instance designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs and 768.0 GB of system memory, establishing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The instance does not include local storage or graphics processing units, meaning storage requirements must be met via network-attached options. By providing direct, non-virtualized access to physical hardware, this bare-metal configuration eliminates hypervisor overhead, making it highly efficient for resource-dense deployments. The server is suited for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise applications, and memory-heavy data processing pipelines that require dedicated CPU and memory resources.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highmem-96-metal is a bare-metal server with 96 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs and 768 GB RAM, designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads without hypervisor overhead.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highmem-96-metal is a bare-metal server featuring 96 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs and 768 GB RAM for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal instance featuring ninety-six dedicated cores and high memory density for demanding enterprise and in-memory database workloads.

768.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Bare-metal deployment eliminating hypervisor virtualization overhead
